items.headline: City launching business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: Cupertino is offering an amnesty program to help unlicensed businesses at shopping centers get into compliance. This could affect local retail operations and how the city enforces business licensing rules.

items.headline: New economic incentives coming for retail and small businesses
items.summary: The city is considering new financial incentives to attract and support retail stores and startups in Cupertino. This could influence what kinds of businesses open or stay in the area.

items.headline: New mixed-use housing development approved at 490 East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View approved a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road on second reading, moving forward with a development agreement. This project will add housing to the city, though specific unit counts and affordability details would matter to residents tracking local housing supply.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive grant funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program, which provides state funding to cities that remove housing barriers. If awarded, this could accelerate housing approvals and construction in Mountain View.

items.headline: City soliciting public input on federal housing grant spending plan
items.summary: Santa Clara is accepting comments on how it will use HUD federal grant money for housing and community development in fiscal year 2026-27. This is your chance to weigh in on local priorities before the plan is finalized.

items.headline: City holds community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale held a public meeting on Monday, March 23 to discuss a safe parking initiative. This suggests the city may be implementing or expanding a program to address vehicle homelessness, which is a visible issue in the South Bay.
